Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE1 : Search Airtable for all April relocation records using airtable_list_bases to find the operations base (appMoveOpsOps001), then airtable_list_tables to find the Relocations table (tblRelocations01), then airtable_list_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Relocations") to pull all records. The agent needs to find 20–30+ April 2026 relocations across six clients (Canopy Health, Vectral Systems, GreenStack Energy, PivotPoint Labs, BrightLoop Analytics, Mosaic Robotics) and identify which moves have "In Progress" or "Completed" statuses, which coordinators are assigned (Fatimah Al-Rashidi, Blessing Okafor, Javier Morales, Suki Patel), and which have special requirements (hazmat, cold-chain, ADA, visa). [Read/lookup action]
OE2: Search Linear for operations and relocation issues using linear_list_teams to find the Operations and Relocations teams, then linear_list_issues (team: "Operations" or "Team Relocations", limit: 50+) to find open tickets for April moves. The agent needs to discover the DOT Compliance Audit project tracking hazmat documentation, the Heartland Q1 reconciliation ticket, and per-move tickets (apartment search, flight booking, moving coordination) with their statuses (todo, in_progress, done). [Read/lookup action]
OE3: Search QuickBooks for invoices with pending surcharges using quickbooks_search_invoices (no filter, or criteria filtering for April 2026 or specific clients). The agent needs to find invoice INV-2026-0411 for Mosaic Robotics ($9,010 total) with $2,450 in non-standard surcharges pending Tamara Byrd's written approval: $1,200 custom prototype crating (Zara Okoye), $800 hazmat handling (Keiko Tanaka), and $450 interim storage (Keiko Tanaka). [Read/lookup action]
OE4: Search Slack for Heartland dispute status using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Heartland dispute" or "Heartland" or similar, filter_in_channel: #operations or #finance). The agent needs to find that the Q1 billing dispute ($4,800 overbilling on invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042) is still unresolved, with a $12,800 payment hold in place. [Read/lookup action]
OE5: Search emails for Swift/Greg Pallone correspondence about the hazmat placarding certificate using search_emails (query: "Swift hazmat" or "Greg Pallone" or "placarding certificate" or similar). The agent needs to discover that Chloe Vance already sent an email to Greg Pallone requesting the signed DOT placarding certificate for Keiko Tanaka's Mosaic laser cutter shipment, and that Greg replied (around April 21–22) confirming the cert exists and promising to send scanned PDFs within 24 hours but as of the current date, the signed PDFs have NOT been received. This means the email to Swift should be framed as a follow-up, not a first request. [Read/lookup action]
OE6: Look up coordinator email addresses using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Fatimah" or "Blessing" or "Javier" or "Suki") or contacts_get_contacts to retrieve fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com, blessing.okafor@moveops.com, javier.morales@moveops.com, suki.patel@moveops.com, and chloe.vance@moveops.com. These are needed as attendees for the calendar event. [Read/lookup action]
OE7: Look up Greg Pallone's email at Swift Relocations using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Greg Pallone" or "Swift") or search_emails to find greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com. This is needed as the recipient for the Swift follow-up email. [Read/lookup action]
OE8: Create tracking tickets in Linear for moves missing critical documentation or vendor confirmation using linear_create_issue (team: "Operations", title, description, assignee, priority). The agent should create tickets for blocked moves it identifies key examples include: Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic (missing DOT placarding cert from Swift), Nadia Okonjo / PivotPoint (H-1B visa attorney coordination unconfirmed), Terrence Nakamura / Vectral (ADA housing not confirmed), Marcus Webb / Canopy (cold-chain cryogenic vendor not confirmed), and others the agent discovers during investigation. Each ticket should be assigned to the responsible coordinator from the Airtable record. [Write action]
OE9: Update stale Airtable relocation records using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01, records: [{id, fields}]). The agent should update records where the status is stale for example, moves with "In Progress" status whose move end dates have already passed, or records missing current status notes. At minimum, the Keiko Tanaka record should be updated with a DOT compliance status note. [Write action]
OE10: Post a full readiness summary to the ops Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: the #operations channel ID, payload: readiness summary). The summary should cover which moves are confirmed, which are blocked or at risk, coordinator workload breakdown, vendor situation (Heartland dispute status, Swift cert status), and pending financial items (Mosaic surcharges). [Write action]
OE11: Schedule a coordinator sync meeting using calendar_add_calendar_event (title: related to "coordinator sync" or "April relocation" or similar, start_datetime: 2026-04-28 10:00:00, end_datetime: 2026-04-28 11:00:00 or later, attendees: ["chloe.vance@moveops.com", "fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com", "blessing.okafor@moveops.com", "javier.morales@moveops.com", "suki.patel@moveops.com"]). The event must include all five attendees and be on April 28, 2026 starting at 10:00 AM Pacific. [Write action]
OE12: Send a follow-up email to Greg Pallone at Swift about the missing hazmat placarding certificate using send_email (sender: chloe.vance@moveops.com, recipients: ["greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com"], subject: related to placarding certificate or Keiko Tanaka or DOT compliance or similar, content: requesting the signed certificate PDFs). The email should reference the Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics laser cutter shipment and the DOT/PHMSA compliance inquiry (Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187). CC'ing marcus.thorne@moveops.com is expected given the DOT financial exposure. [Write action]

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The model must use a QuickBooks tool (e.g., quickbooks_search_invoices, quickbooks_search_bills, or quickbooks_search_accounts) to search for invoice or billing data
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓— 100%
✓Run #1The agent called `mcp__mcp_baa6ae1a__quickbooks_search_invoices` with no filter (empty input `{}`), which returns all invoices including the Mosaic Robotics invoice with surcharges.
✓Run #2The agent called `quickbooks_search_invoices` with `criteria: null` (broad/unfiltered search) AND `quickbooks_search_bills` with `fetchAll: true`, both of which would return invoice/billing data including the Mosaic surcharges.
✓Run #3The agent called `quickbooks_search_invoices` with an empty input `{}` (no filter), which would return all invoices including Mosaic's. This satisfies the requirement to use a QuickBooks tool to search for invoice/billing data.
✓Run #4The agent called `quickbooks_search_invoices` with empty input `{}` early in the trajectory, which returns all invoices with no filter — sufficient to capture the Mosaic invoice and any pending surcharges.
✓Run #5The agent called `quickbooks_search_invoices` with an empty input (broad search) early in the trajectory, satisfying the requirement to use a QuickBooks tool to search for invoice/billing data.
—Run #6No justification
2 A calendar event was created (via calendar_add_calendar_event) with a start date of April 28, 2026 and a start time of 10:00 AM Pacific.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓— 100%
✓Run #1The agent called `calendar_add_calendar_event` with `start_datetime: '2026-04-28 10:00:00'`, matching the required date and time. The tool returned a successful event_id.
✓Run #2The agent called `calendar_add_calendar_event` with `start_datetime: '2026-04-28 10:00:00'` and the tool returned success with event_id `fe039d174d714ff8b156022939628822`. The date and time match the requirement exactly.
✓Run #3The agent called `calendar_add_calendar_event` with `start_datetime: '2026-04-28 10:00:00'` and the tool returned a successful event_id (`519c42d901394cecaca665e29e49453f`). The date and time match exactly.
✓Run #4The agent called `calendar_add_calendar_event` with `start_datetime: '2026-04-28 10:00:00'` and the tool returned a success event_id. The event description references '10:00 AM Pacific.' Date and time match the requirement.
✓Run #5The agent called `calendar_add_calendar_event` with start_datetime '2026-04-28 10:00:00', which matches April 28, 2026 at 10:00 AM. The tool returned a successful event_id.
—Run #6No justification
3 An email was sent (via send_email) from [chloe.vance@moveops.com](mailto:chloe.vance@moveops.com) to [greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com](mailto:greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com)
outcome

✓Run #3The agent called `send_email` with `sender: 'chloe.vance@moveops.com'` and `recipients: ['greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com']`. The tool returned a successful email_id (`e029e25f9374437a8d56e6ee0fc080b1`).

4 The email body (visible in send_email parameters) to [greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com](mailto:greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com) references the Keiko Tanaka relocation for Mosaic Robotics.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓— 100%
✓Run #1The email body explicitly names 'Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics' in the subject and body, and references the Epilog Fusion Pro 48 laser cutter shipment and the need for the signed DOT hazmat placarding certificate.
✓Run #2The email subject explicitly mentions 'Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics laser cutter' and the email body references the DOT placarding certificate in the context of the Mosaic laser cutter shipment (Swift Job #SR-2026-04-1538).
✓Run #3The email subject and body both explicitly reference 'Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics laser cutter shipment (Columbus, OH to Seattle, WA — Swift job #SR-2026-04-1538)' and the need for a 'signed DOT hazmat placarding certificate.'
✓Run #4The email subject explicitly includes 'Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics' and the body references 'the Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics laser cutter shipment (Columbus, OH to Seattle, WA)' and the placarding certificate.

5 The email body (visible in send_email parameters) to [greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com](mailto:greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com) references the DOT or PHMSA compliance inquiry (e.g., 'PHMSA-2026-04-1187' or 'DOT audit' or 'PHMSA inquiry' or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓— 100%
✓Run #1The email body references both DOT (49 CFR 172.504, 49 CFR 107.329) and PHMSA Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187, fulfilling the DOT/PHMSA compliance inquiry reference requirement.
✓Run #2The email body explicitly references 'Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187' and 'DOT response package', satisfying the requirement for DOT/PHMSA compliance inquiry reference.
✓Run #3The email body explicitly references 'a formal PHMSA compliance inquiry (Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187)' and penalty exposure under '49 CFR 107.329', satisfying the DOT/PHMSA compliance criterion.
✓Run #4The email body explicitly references 'PHMSA Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187,' 'DOT compliance response package,' and '49 CFR 172, Subpart F' — all clearly satisfying the DOT/PHMSA compliance reference requirement.
✓Run #5The email body explicitly references 'PHMSA Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187' and cites '49 CFR 107.329' penalties, clearly referencing the DOT/PHMSA compliance inquiry.
—Run #6No justification
6 A message was posted (via conversations_add_message) to the #operations Slack channel (channel ID C006 or channel name containing 'operations').
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓— 100%
✓Run #1The agent called `conversations_add_message` with `channel_id: '#operations'`. The tool returned a successful result.
✓Run #2The agent called `conversations_add_message` with `channel_id: '#operations'`. The tool returned success with an output file confirming the message was posted.
✓Run #3The agent called `conversations_add_message` with `channel_id: '#operations'` and the tool returned a successful result (output file confirms success). The channel matches the #operations requirement.

—Run #6No justification
7 The Slack message posted to #operations (visible in conversations_add_message payload) identifies at least 3 specific April relocations by employee name that are blocked, at risk, or missing documentation.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✗— 80%
✓Run #1The Slack message explicitly names at least 3 blocked/at-risk employees: Keiko Tanaka (BLOCKED), Marcus Webb/Canopy (BLOCKED), Terrence Nakamura (AT RISK), Nadia Okonjo (AT RISK), Diego Castañeda (AT RISK), and Dr. Renata Kovacs (AT RISK).
✓Run #2The Slack message payload explicitly names at least 10 employees with blocked/at-risk status, including Simone Richter, Marcus Webb (BrightLoop), Jae-won Kim as BLOCKED, and 7 additional verification-needed moves with named employees (Kovacs, Cruz, Okonjo, Nakamura, Castañeda, Zhao, etc.).
✓Run #3The Slack message names many specific blocked/at-risk employees including Noah Fitzgerald, Simone Richter, Marcus Webb, Keiko Tanaka, Mei-Lin Zhao, Dr. Renata Kovacs, Nadia Okonjo, and Terrence Nakamura — well exceeding the threshold of 3 blocked/at-risk moves by name.
✓Run #4The Slack message explicitly names Keiko Tanaka, Simone Richter, Marcus Webb, Jae-won Kim, Emilia Cruz, Raj Patel, and Deshawn Hartley — all identified as blocked or at-risk. Well exceeds the minimum of 3.
